title: Norn session loop — async agent lifecycle
purpose: Implement the async Norn session loop that replaces the blocking Claude Runner session loop for Norn-powered sessions. This is the core runtime integration — it builds a Norn Agent, runs it via tokio::spawn, translates events into AssistantEvent, and handles session commands through Norn's native mechanisms.
task:
Implement run_norn_session_loop in norn_session.rs and wire spawn_norn_loop in service.rs to call it. The loop multiplexes four event sources: Norn AgentEvent broadcast, SessionCommand mpsc, idle timeout, and agent completion. Commands are delivered via InboundChannel (SendMessage) and CancellationToken (Stop/Kill). The completion watcher emits lifecycle events on the global EventBus. This brief replaces the NR-001 stub.

Dependency setup: No new cargo dependencies. The norn crate is already a dependency of meridian-services (norn = { path = "../norn" }). All required tokio features (sync, time, rt-multi-thread) are already present.

requirements.title: norn_session.rs module with run_norn_session_loop
requirements.spec: The system SHALL define an async function run_norn_session_loop in assistant/norn_session.rs. It SHALL accept an AgentBuilder, session_id, broadcast::Sender<AssistantEvent>, mpsc::Receiver<SessionCommand>, and optional idle timeout Duration. It SHALL be spawned via tokio::spawn (not spawn_blocking).
acceptance: norn_session.rs exists in crates/meridian-services/src/assistant/
run_norn_session_loop is async and accepts the specified parameters
mod.rs exports the new module
The function runs on the tokio async runtime, not a blocking thread

requirements.title: AgentBuilder construction with InboundChannel
requirements.spec: WHEN spawn_norn_loop is called, THE SYSTEM SHALL build an AgentBuilder with: provider (from build_norn_provider or TokenPool-sourced auth), session EventStore (from NR-004), AgentEventSender for event streaming, CancellationToken for shutdown, the resolved Profile, and an InboundChannel for mid-session message delivery. The InboundSender half of the channel SHALL be stored on the SessionHandle so the session loop can receive messages. THE SYSTEM SHALL call Agent::run_with(prompt) where prompt is the initial_message from SessionConfig.
acceptance: AgentBuilder is constructed with provider, event sender, cancellation token, profile, and InboundChannel
InboundSender is stored on SessionHandle for later SendMessage delivery
Agent::run_with is called with the initial_message text
The agent runs as an async task within the norn session loop
InboundChannel is created by the server (not inherited from sub-agent machinery)

requirements.title: Event multiplexing select loop
requirements.spec: The norn session loop SHALL use tokio::select! to multiplex: (a) AgentEvent from broadcast::Receiver — translated via NornTranslateState and forwarded to the session broadcast, (b) SessionCommand from mpsc::Receiver — dispatched to InboundChannel or CancellationToken, (c) idle timeout — fires after configured duration with no activity, (d) agent join handle — resolves when the Norn agent completes.
acceptance: tokio::select! multiplexes all four sources
AgentEvent events are translated and forwarded to broadcast::Sender<AssistantEvent>
SessionCommand messages dispatch correctly
Idle timeout branch stops the session
Agent completion branch exits the loop

requirements.title: SendMessage bridge to InboundChannel with DeliveryMode
requirements.spec: WHEN SessionCommand::SendMessage(text) is received, THE SYSTEM SHALL construct a ChannelMessage from the text, send it via InboundSender with DeliveryMode::Steer (injected at next tool boundary, interrupts current work), AND emit AssistantEvent::UserMessage on the session broadcast. DMs use DeliveryMode::Steer because they are intentional communication requiring attention. Notifications (if routed through a future path) SHOULD use DeliveryMode::FollowUp (surfaces only if agent would otherwise stop).
acceptance: SendMessage constructs a ChannelMessage with the text content
ChannelMessage is sent via InboundSender (the sender half stored on SessionHandle)
DeliveryMode::Steer is used for DM-originated messages
AssistantEvent::UserMessage is emitted with the text on the session broadcast
Idle timeout timer is reset on SendMessage
If InboundSender is closed (agent completed), SendMessage returns an error instead of silently dropping

requirements.title: Stop and Kill via CancellationToken
requirements.spec: WHEN SessionCommand::Stop is received, THE SYSTEM SHALL cancel the CancellationToken and drain remaining events until the agent completes. WHEN SessionCommand::Kill is received, THE SYSTEM SHALL cancel the CancellationToken and exit the loop immediately without draining.
acceptance: Stop sets a stopping flag, cancels the token, and continues draining events
Kill cancels the token and breaks from the loop immediately
Both emit AssistantEvent::StatusChanged with SessionStatus::Stopping then Stopped

requirements.title: Idle timeout with had_activity gate
requirements.spec: WHEN an idle timeout is configured AND the agent has completed at least one turn (had_activity is true), THE SYSTEM SHALL stop the session after the timeout duration elapses with no events. The timer SHALL reset on every AgentEvent and every SendMessage command. WHEN had_activity is false, the timeout SHALL NOT fire.
acceptance: Idle timeout uses tokio::time::sleep, not crossbeam::after
Timer resets on AgentEvent receipt and SendMessage receipt
Timer does not fire before the first turn completes (had_activity gate)
Timeout causes session stop with SessionExitStatus::IdleTimeout equivalent

requirements.title: Session completion and status transitions
requirements.spec: WHEN the Norn agent's run_with join handle resolves with Ok, THE SYSTEM SHALL emit StatusChanged(Stopped), mark the session as completed in the registry, emit ServiceEvent::SessionCompleted on the global EventBus, mark the agent session finished in PG, and release the member's wake claim. WHEN run_with returns Err (provider auth failure, permanent error), THE SYSTEM SHALL emit StatusChanged(Stopped) and ServiceEvent::SessionFailed with the error message. WHEN run_with panics, THE SYSTEM SHALL emit ServiceEvent::SessionFailed. All three paths SHALL release the member's wake claim.
acceptance: Agent completion (Ok) emits StatusChanged(Stopped) + ServiceEvent::SessionCompleted
Agent error (Err) emits StatusChanged(Stopped) + ServiceEvent::SessionFailed with error message
Agent panic emits ServiceEvent::SessionFailed
All three paths (Ok, Err, panic) release wake claim by setting member activity to Available
All three paths mark the agent session finished in PG
SessionRegistry is updated via mark_completed in all cases

dependency_setup: No new cargo dependencies. The norn crate is already a dependency of meridian-services (norn = { path = "../norn" }). All required tokio features (sync, time, rt-multi-thread) are already present.
boundaries: SHALL NOT implement event translation logic (NR-003 scope — this brief calls NornTranslateState but does not define it)
SHALL NOT implement session file management (NR-004 scope — this brief receives an EventStore but does not create it)
SHALL NOT implement namespace tools (NR-006 scope)
SHALL NOT modify the Claude Runner session loop (session_loop.rs)
SHALL NOT modify ws/assistant.rs, persistence.rs, or streaming.rs
verification: cargo check --workspace passes with norn dependency added to meridian-services Cargo.toml
spawn_norn_loop replaces the NR-001 stub and constructs a real Norn session
SessionCommand::SendMessage delivers text through the Norn path
Idle timeout fires after configured duration with had_activity gate
